benchmark_protocol.schedule: Balanced round-robin rotation with reversed cycles; each 1/2/4-sheet variant appears equally often in every position.
benchmark_protocol.representative_policy: Measured run nearest total_editor_ms median; earliest run breaks ties.
benchmark_protocol.compatibility: The representative output for every variant was opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and checked for sheet names, edited values and dimensions.
benchmark_protocol.timing_scope: Source preparation, report serialization and OpenPyXL validation are outside benchmark timing and process CPU telemetry. OS file/page caches were not cleared.

validation.same_revision_production_ctest: 133/133 passed before commit and build-resource cleanup

artifact_capture: The runner reports retain their generic statement that a local report is not tracked evidence by itself. This directory's validated manifest, artifact hashes and claim-to-artifact mapping are the authority that promotes these unchanged reports into tracked evidence without widening their local-workload qualification.
resource_lifecycle: Benchmark sources, per-run JSON and XLSX outputs were kept only under build/qa until the two matrix reports were captured and hashed. No background worker was introduced by this profiling run.
qualification: Claims are limited to the recorded same-machine fixed-shape workloads. Four worksheets contain four times the source cells and edits of one worksheet. This bundle does not establish cross-machine, fixed-total, arbitrary-workbook, Office, concurrency, backend or full-feature claims.
